items.headline: Dallas Stars not breaking up roster after early playoff exit
items.description:
There will be no overhaul of the Dallas Stars this offseason, general manager Jim Nill said Thursday.
Nill said he likes the makeup of his team despite losing to the Minnesota Wild in six games in the Western Conference First Round, which followed three straight trips to the Western Conference

items.description:

items.headline: Norris Trophy finalists for best defenseman for 2025-26 announced by NHL
items.description:
Rasmus Dahlin, Cale Makar and Zach Werenski were named finalists for the Norris Trophy on Thursday.
The winner of the award, given annually to the defenseman voted the best at his position by the Professional Hockey Writers Association, will be announced at a later date.

items.headline: NHL EDGE stats leaders for 2025-2026 season
items.description:
NHL.com's fantasy staff continues to cover the latest trends and storylines in the League through the lens of NHL EDGE puck and player tracker stats. Throughout the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs, we will identify the leaders in key advanced metrics.

items.headline: NHL EDGE stats: Nathan MacKinnon Conn Smythe 2026 contender
items.description: NHL.com's fantasy staff continues to cover the latest trends and storylines in the League through the lens of NHL EDGE puck and player tracker stats. Today, we provide the underlying metrics behind Nathan MacKinnon of the Colorado Avalanche.

items.headline: Noah Cates injury status update May 6 2026
items.description:
VOORHEES, N.J. -- Noah Cates will miss the remainder of the Eastern Conference Second Round for the Philadelphia Flyers because of a lower-body injury.
The center sustained the injury during a 3-2 overtime loss at the Carolina Hurricanes in Game 2 on Monday. He played 20:53, including 3:23 during overtime.

items.headline: Beckett Sennecke giving Anaheim spark, swagger in postseason run
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- Swagger.
The Anaheim Ducks use that word often to describe forward Beckett Sennecke.
Off the ice, the 20-year-old will give it right back when a teammate chirps him, whether he's getting it from another young player or a veteran.
"He's a little bit of a clown, but

items.headline: Stanley Cup Playoffs Playback for May 7, 2026
items.description: The Stanley Cup Playoffs are in full swing and NHL.com has it all covered. Each day, we will present Stanley Cup Playoffs Playback, your one-stop shop for all the action. Here’s a look at where things stand on Thursday, May 7:

items.headline: Scott Tassone voted 2026 Most Valuable Teacher Award by Tampa Bay Lightning
items.description:
The Future Goals Most Valuable Teacher program is part of the NHL and NHLPA's Future Goals initiative and is funded by their joint Industry Growth Fund (IGF).
To Scott Tassone, the pep rally at Oakstead Elementary School in Land O’ Lakes, Florida in April was simply a way to celebrate

items.headline: Anaheim Ducks get up to speed, shut down Vegas Golden Knights
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- The Anaheim Ducks are supposed to be a speedy offensive team.
Well, in Game 2 of the Western Conference Second Round on Wednesday, they were a speedy defensive team.
They smothered the Vegas Golden Knights in what coach Joel Quenneville called one of their better defensive performances

items.headline: Anaheim Ducks Vegas Golden Knights Game 2 recap May 6, 2026
items.description: LAS VEGAS -- Lukas Dostal made 21 saves for the Anaheim Ducks in a 3-1 win against the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 2 of the Western Conference Second Round at T-Mobile Arena on Monday.

items.headline: Anaheim Ducks fans revel in ‘exciting’ Game 2 win at watch party
items.description:
ANAHEIM -- Rocking a duck mask and a Mason McTavish jersey, Ryan Quibodeaux made a bold prediction for the Anaheim Ducks in the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs.
“They’re going to win it all,” Quibodeaux said at the Anaheim Ducks Playoff Watch Party presented by Ticketmaster at Honda Center on Wednesday.

items.headline: Montreal Canadiens tested by slow start in Game 1
items.description:
BUFFALO — Lane Hutson, like the rest of the Montreal Canadiens, wanted to get off to a good start in the best-of-7 Eastern Conference Second Round against the Buffalo Sabres Wednesday.
They did anything but.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs second round schedule television results
items.description: The second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs began Saturday with the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0. The second round continued on Sunday with the Colorado Avalanche defeating the Minnesota Wild 9-6.
